debug_info.goal:
C : Type
inst✝¹ : Category.{0, 0} C
K : Type
inst✝ : DivisionRing K
M : CategoryTheory.Functor C (ModuleCat K)
D : @Dual.DirectSumDecomposition C inst✝¹ K inst✝ M
B : (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) → N ∈ D → Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
hB : ∀ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hN : N ∈ D), N = sSup (B N hN)
hB' : ∀ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hN : N ∈ D), sSupIndep (B N hN)
hB'' : ∀ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hN : N ∈ D), ⊥ ∉ B N hN
⊢ sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) = ⊤

failed_attempts:
rw [h₆]
                <;> simp
              -- Use the fact that the pointwise supremum is the supremum of the submodules.
              have h₈ : (sSup (SetLike.coe D)) x ≤ ⨆ p ∈ (SetLike.coe D), p x := by
                -- Use the fact that the pointwise supremum is the supremum of the submodules.
                apply le_iSup
                intro p hp
                exact le_iSup_of_le hp (le_sup hp)
              -- Combine the inequalities to get the desired result.
              calc
                (⊤ : Module.k K (M x)) = (sSup (SetLike.coe D)) x := by rw [h₇]
                _ ≤ ⨆ p ∈ (SetLike.coe D), p x := h₈
            exact h₅
        exact h₃
      exact h₂

have h₂ : sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) = ⊤ := by
      calc
        sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) ≥ sSup (SetLike.coe D) := by
          -- Use the fact that each N in D is the supremum of B N hN
          have h₃ : SetLike.coe D ⊆ ⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN := by
            intro N hN
            have h₄ : N = sSup (B N hN) := hB N hN
            have h₅ : N ∈ SetLike.coe D := by simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
            have h₆ : N ∈ ⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN := by
              -- Show that N is in the union of B N hN
              have h₇ : N ∈ ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN := by
                have h₈ : N ∈ D := by simpa [Set.mem_setOf_eq] using h₅
                have h₉ : N ∈ (⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) := by
                  exact Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨h₈, Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨h₈, by simp⟩⟩
                exact h₉
              have h₁₀ : ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN ⊆ ⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN := by
                apply Set.iUnion_subset_iUnion
                intro _ h₁₁
                apply Set.subset_univ
              exact h₁₀ h₇
            exact h₆
          exact sSup_le_sSup h₃
        _ = ⊤ := by
          -- Use the fact that the supremum of D is ⊤
          have h₃ : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
          simp_all
        _ = ⊤ := by rfl
    exact h₂
  not_bot_mem' := by
    -- Prove that ⊥ is not in the carrier
    intro h
    -- Extract the contradiction from the given conditions
    have h₁ : ⊥ ∈ (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) := h
    have h₂ : ∃ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hN : N ∈ D) (B' : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), ⊥ ∈ B' := by
      -- Use the fact that ⊥ is in the union of B N hN
      simp only [Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_iUnion] at h₁
      obtain ⟨N, hN, B', hB', hB''⟩ := h₁
      refine' ⟨N, hN, B', _⟩
      aesop
    obtain ⟨N, hN, B', hB', hB''⟩ := h₂
    have h₃ : ⊥ ∉ B N hN := hB'' N hN
    have h₄ : ⊥ ∈ B' := by aesop
    have h₅ : B' ⊆ B N hN := by aesop
    have h₆ : ⊥ ∈ B N hN := h₅ h₄
    contradiction
  <;> aesop

ext x
            simp only [Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.mem_coe]
            <;>
            aesop
          rw [h₆] at *
          exact h₅
        _ = ⊤ := by rw [h₁]
